frameworks.name: diffusion of working memory representations
frameworks.one_line: Latent memory trace drifts over delay, thresholded match/non-match decision plus lapses.

frameworks.name: model-based behavioral psychophysics
frameworks.one_line: Fit individual behavior with a computational latent-dynamics model.

frameworks.name: persistent stimulus-selective cortical activity
frameworks.one_line: Distributed delay-period activity assumed as substrate of working memory.

core_question: What computational and neural mechanisms underlie working memory decline in healthy aging and mild cognitive impairment?
key_assumptions: Working memory contents are maintained by stimulus-selective persistent activity across distributed cortex.
Memory representations diffuse stochastically across the delay period.
Behavior arises from a threshold on the diffused trace plus an independent lapse process.
manipulations: Temporal delay between sample and test in a visuo-spatial delayed-match-to-sample task.
Spatial distance between sample and test stimulus locations.
Group: younger adults vs. older healthy controls vs. older adults with MCI.
key_findings: Behavior on the delayed-match-to-sample task was captured by a latent diffusion + threshold + lapse model fit per individual.
Older adults with MCI were compared to healthy older controls using model-based psychophysics combined with MEG.
Younger adults provided a behavioral reference, characterized with the same computational model.
interpretations: Working memory in aging and MCI can be dissected into latent dynamic components (diffusion, decision threshold, lapses) linked to cortical activity.
Combining computational modeling with MEG offers a path to relate cognitive integrity to working-memory dynamics in the aging brain.
